messages.content:
When does a policy lapse happen?

 -The policy period ends
 -Act of God
 -Policyholder fails to pay the premium
 -All of the above

give me the answer
All of the above.

Insurance Industry

What are the alternatives to underwriting ?

 A: Accept the submission
 B: Reject the submission
 C: Frame underwriting policy and revise guidelines
 Both A and B
C: Frame underwriting policy and revise guidelines

Which one of these is not a benefit provided by the insurance company ?

 Disability benefit
 Surrender Benefit
 Micro Insurance Benefit
 Health Benefit
Surrender Benefit

Identify different types of property loss

 Loss or damage to the article itself
 Loss of income from the use of the article
 The extra expense incurred due to the loss of the article
 All of the above
All of the above

Which of these tools are not used in the Insurance Claims Evaluation phase?

 Physical damage estimating tool
 Claim routing tool
 Bodily injury valuation tool
 Medical management tool
Claim routing tool

Which of the following is not a test tool used under ATCI guidewire delivery tools ?

 Service Now
 TOSCA
 Ranorex
 Selenium
Service Now

Select the correct statement for no claim bonus

 A special bonus is given on Annuity amount
 Increase in the amount of the deductibles for no claim
 A special discount is given on premium for every claim-free year
 None of the above
A special discount is given on premium for every claim-free year

Umbrella insurance is ____

 a type of reinsurance
 an underwriting alternative
 extra liability insurance
 a contract where the insurer makes payments to you, either immediately or in future
extra liability insurance

Select the correct order in the claims process: 1. Recovery 2. Notification 3. Settlement 4. Evaluation

 2-3-4-1
 2-4-3-1
 4-1-2-3
 4-2-1-3

Who is a "Loss Payee" in the process of Insurance Claim?
Please select one answer below:

Loss Payee is the person who pays the Premium for the Insurance	
Loss Payee is the person who pays for the loss	
The loss payee is a person or institution (Bank) that receives the insurance payment on the loss of the property.	
All of the above
The loss payee is a person or institution (Bank) that receives the insurance payment on the loss of the property.

What does Personal Accident coverage provide?
Please select one answer below:

Accidental Death coverage	
Natural Death Coverage	
Both A & B	
None of the above
Accidental Death coverage

Who is a Broker?
Please select one answer below:

An Insurance Broker is an Insurance Agent	
Insurance Brokers work only for one Insurance Company	
Insurance Brokers are not a worldwide concept	
An insurance broker is a specialist in insurance and risk management. Brokers act on behalf of their clients and provide advice in the interests of their clients.
An insurance broker is a specialist in insurance and risk management. Brokers act on behalf of their clients and provide advice in the interests of their clients.

When an Insured is brought back to the same position just before a loss, which principle is this scenario indicating?
Please select one answer below:

Principle of Proximate Cause	
Principle of Indemnity	
Principle of Insurable Interest	
Principle of Utmost Good Faith
Principle of Indemnity

What is a Premium?
Please select one answer below:

Amount to be paid by the Insured for a contract of insurance to the insurance company	
Amount the Insurer pays to the Insured as proof of Insurance Contract	
Claim Amount that is being settled for a loss sustained by the Insured	
None of the above
Amount to be paid by the Insured for a contract of insurance to the insurance company.

What are the major types of Insurance available in the market ?
Please select one answer below:

Property & Casualty Insurance	
Life Insurance	
Accident & Health Insurance	
All of the above
All of the above

Who is an Agent?
Please select one answer below:

An Agent is person who acts on behalf of the customer	
An insurance agent is a person who works for an insurance company and sells the insurance products of that company	
An Agent is appointed for collection of recoveries by the Insurance company	
None of the above
An insurance agent is a person who works for an insurance company and sells the insurance products of that company.

While all contracts are contracts of Good Faith, however, Insurance Contracts are Contracts of _________________ !
Please select one answer below:

Offer and Acceptance	
Act of God Faith	
Utmost Good Faith	
Insurance does not work on faith
Utmost Good Faith

What is a Deductible?
Please select one answer below:

Deductible is reduction in Premium	
Deductible is the least Claim amount payable	
Deductible is a decided amount that the Insured has to pay from their pocket while claiming Insurance.	
Deductible is not practiced in Insurance
Deductible is a decided amount that the Insured has to pay from their pocket while claiming Insurance.

What is a No Claim Bonus?
Please select one answer below:

No Claim Bonus is an yearly Bonus that is paid as part of Salary for an employee of an Insurance Company	
No claim bonus is a benefit for an Insured who has not claimed insurance during the preceding year of cover.	
No Claim Bonus is a health benefit scheme provided by the government	
No Claim Bonus is a benefit that is provided to a claimant
No claim bonus is a benefit for an Insured who has not claimed insurance during the preceding year of cover.

Premium base for Workers Compensation rating is
Please select one answer below:

Number of employees	
Age of owner	
Payroll	
Forms of ownership
Payroll

Exposure to liability for injury or damage due to activities in addition to the ownership, occupancy, or use of premises
Please select one answer below:

Products Liability Exposure	
Completed Operations Liability Exposure	
Operations Liability Exposure	
Premises Liability Exposure
Operations Liability Exposure

What is : The contract between the shipper and the carrier that may limit the shipper's recovery for cargo loss
Please select one answer below:

Premium	
Bill of lading	
Policy Declaration	
Shipment Documents
Bill of lading

A general classification of insurance, such as Commercial Property, Commercial General Liability, Commercial Crime is called
Please select one answer below:

Standard Classification	
Non standard classification	
Book of Business	
Line of Business
Line of Business

The party temporarily possessing the personal property in a bailment is
Please select one answer below:

Bailor	
Bailee	
Mortgagee	
Mortgagor
Bailee

Any intangible property that consists of human knowledge and idea is
Please select one answer below:

Personal Property	
Intellectual Property	
Movable property	
Real Property
Intellectual Property

Customer's business classification which best describes an insured's business activity is called
Please select one answer below:

Class Code	
Judgement Rating	
Governing Classification	
Special Rating
Class Code

The unlawful taking of property from care and custody of a person by one who has caused or threatened to cause that person bodily harm
Please select one answer below:

Robbery	
Safe Burglary	
Theft	
Accident
Robbery

The unit in which the exposure is measured such as gross sales or payroll is
Please select one answer below:

Rate	
Premium Amount	
Premium Base	
Classification
Premium Base

The compensation a carrier receives for transporting cargo is
Please select one answer below:

Premium	
Reimbursement	
Freight	
Deductible
Freight

Insurance that covers for profit businesses or not-for-profit organizations against the adverse financial effects of property and liability losses is
Please select one answer below:

Personal Insurance	
Commercial Insurance	
Life Insurance	
Health Insurance
Commercial Insurance

Probable Maximum Loss (PML) is
Please select one answer below:

The largest loss that an insured is likely to sustain	
The maximum loss amount paid by Insurance carrier in event of loss	
Maximum loss paid by third party at failed	
All of the above
The largest loss that an insured is likely to sustain

In an insurance agency relationship, the agents fundamental responsibility is to act for the benefit of the
Please select one answer below:

Insurer	
Broker	
Insured	
policyholder
Insured

A numeric code representing the description in the rating classification table that best fits a particular organization's operations is
Please select one answer below:

Premium	
Insured	
Premium Base	
Class Code
Class Code

Policy that covers only one line of business is called
Please select one answer below:

Monoline Policy	
Single ended policy	
Open Policy	
Closed Policy
Monoline Policy

Which of the following is not a Commercial Insurance Line of Business?
Please select one answer below:

Workers Compensation	
Farm Insurance	
Home Insurance	
Inland Marine Insurance
Home Insurance

Unlike in the individual insurance contracts, the underwriting process in group contracts ------------------.
Please select one answer below:

Requires every individual to present the evidence of his insurability	
Underwriting is done based on the characteristics of the employer	
Doesn't require every individual to present the evidence of his insurability
Doesn't require every individual to present the evidence of his insurability

In a Fixed Annuity,
Please select one answer below:

The payout depends on investment returns	
The Fixed payout is guaranteed	
The payouts can be fixed or variable
The Fixed payout is guaranteed

All of the following documents should be kept indefinitely, EXCEPT:
Please select one answer below:

Employee’s salary history	
Plan financial reports	
Plan document	
Amount of benefits paid
Employee’s salary history

The group insurance underwriting guidelines usually require that for a group to be eligible for coverage, the group must have been formed for a reason other than _________.
Please select one answer below:

obtaining group insurance	
not obtaining group insurance	
obtaining individual insurance	
not obtaining individual insurance
obtaining group insurance

The person whose lifetime is used to determine the amount of benefits payable under an annuity contract is known as:
Please select one answer below:

The annuitant	
Payee	
Contract Owner	
Annuity Consideration
The annuitant

The value of Variable Annuity depends on:
Please select one answer below:

The performance of Stocks & Bonds	
The interest rates	
The accumulation period
The performance of Stocks & Bonds

Who is not a Pension provider?
Please select one answer below:

Insurance Company	
Employer	
Trust	
Employee
Employee
